title: Avoiding Micro-Frontend Friction
content: Micro-frontends fail when boundaries are fuzzy. Teams need clear ownership for runtime shell responsibilities versus domain app responsibilities.
The most useful step is defining contracts early: routing handoff, shared auth behavior, and observability shape. These contracts should be tiny and versioned.
For day-to-day delivery, keep the platform path paved. If the default setup is easy, product teams follow it. If setup is heavy, every team forks local solutions.
When coordination gets hard, reduce coupling before adding tooling. Most friction disappears when teams can ship independently with minimal synchronization.
